title: baccharoides calvoana subsp. ruwenzoriensis
intro: Baccharoides calvoana subsp. ruwenzoriensis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known for its medicinal properties. This plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids in its extracts. The health benefits of Baccharoides calvoana subsp. ruwenzoriensis include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its analgesic and antipyretic effects. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of decoctions or infusions, and sometimes combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
